contract_claim: The document describes the filtered variables as including '<>t__*' and '<>s__*' (async state machine fields), but the shadow doc for adapter-policy-dotnet.ts only describes three variable filter patterns: prefix '<>' (C# generated, L90-92), prefix 'CS$<>' (closure variables, L95-97), and prefix '$VB$' (VB.NET generated, L100-102). The patterns '<>t__*' and '<>s__*' are not separately listed — they would be caught by the general '<>' prefix filter. The doc misleadingly lists them as separate filter patterns and also incorrectly describes '<>c__DisplayClass*' as a separate filter (it's also caught by the '<>' prefix). Additionally, the doc labels 'CS$<>*' as 'compiler temporaries' but the shadow doc calls them 'Closure variables'.
observed_behavior: C# generated: prefix `<>` (L90–92), Closure variables: prefix `CS$<>` (L95–97), VB.NET generated: prefix `$VB$` (L100–102)
